heading: Purpose of grants
label: 290cc–22 Purpose of grants

children.label: A
children.text: are suffering from serious mental illness; or

children.label: B
children.text: are suffering from serious mental illness and from a substance use disorder; and
are homeless or at imminent risk of becoming homeless.
outreach services;
screening and diagnostic treatment services;
habilitation and rehabilitation services;
community mental health services;
alcohol or drug treatment services;
staff training, including the training of individuals who work in shelters, mental health clinics, substance use disorder programs, and other sites where homeless individuals require services;

children.label: A
children.text: preparing a plan for the provision of community mental health services to the eligible homeless individual involved, and reviewing such plan not less than once every 3 months;

children.label: B
children.text: providing assistance in obtaining and coordinating social and maintenance services for the eligible homeless individuals, including services relating to daily living activities, personal financial planning, transportation services, and habilitation and rehabilitation services, prevocational and vocational services, and housing services;

children.label: C
children.text: providing assistance to the eligible homeless individual in obtaining income support services, including housing assistance, supplemental nutrition assistance program benefits, and supplemental security income benefits;

children.label: D
children.text: referring the eligible homeless individual for such other services as may be appropriate; and

children.label: E
children.text: providing representative payee services in accordance with section 1631(a)(2) of the Social Security Act [] if the eligible homeless individual is receiving aid under title XVI of such act [ et seq.] and if the applicant is designated by the Secretary to provide such services;42 U.S.C. 1383(a)(2)42 U.S.C. 1381
supportive and supervisory services in residential settings;
referrals for primary health services, job training, educational services, and relevant housing services;

children.label: A
children.text: minor renovation, expansion, and repair of housing;

children.label: B
children.text: planning of housing;

children.label: C
children.text: technical assistance in applying for housing assistance;

children.label: D
children.text: improving the coordination of housing services;

children.label: E
children.text: security deposits;

children.label: F
children.text: the costs associated with matching eligible homeless individuals with appropriate housing situations; and

children.label: G
children.text: 1-time rental payments to prevent eviction; and
other appropriate services, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: c Coordination
children.text: The Secretary may not make payments under unless the State involved agrees to make grants pursuant to subsection (a) only to entities that have the capacity to provide, directly or through arrangements, the services specified in subsection (b), including coordinating the provision of services in order to meet the needs of eligible homeless individuals who are both mentally ill and suffering from a substance use disorder.section 290cc–21 of this title

children.heading: Special consideration regarding veterans
children.label: d Special consideration regarding veterans
children.text: The Secretary may not make payments under unless the State involved agrees that, in making grants to entities pursuant to subsection (a), the State will give special consideration to entities with a demonstrated effectiveness in serving homeless veterans.section 290cc–21 of this title
has a policy of excluding individuals from mental health services due to the existence or suspicion of a substance use disorder; or
has a policy of excluding individuals from substance use disorder services due to the existence or suspicion of mental illness.

children.heading: Administrative expenses
children.label: f Administrative expenses
children.text: The Secretary may not make payments under unless the State involved agrees that not more than 4 percent of the payments will be expended for administrative expenses regarding the payments.section 290cc–21 of this title

children.heading: Restrictions on use of funds
children.label: g Restrictions on use of funds

children.text: not more than 20 percent of the payments will be expended for housing services under subsection (b)(10); and

children.label: A
children.text: to support emergency shelters or construction of housing facilities;

children.label: B
children.text: for inpatient psychiatric treatment costs or inpatient substance use disorder treatment costs; or

children.label: C
children.text: to make cash payments to intended recipients of mental health or substance use disorder services.

children.heading: Waiver for territories
children.label: h Waiver for territories
children.text: With respect to the United States Virgin Islands, Guam, American Samoa, Palau, the Marshall Islands, and the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, the Secretary may waive the provisions of this part that the Secretary determines to be appropriate.
